title: Armed Group Attacks Mexican Army Patrol in Escuinapa — 4 Detained, 3 Are Minors
detail: Cartel gunmen ambushed a Mexican Army (SEDENA) ground patrol in Escuinapa municipality on April 5, 2026. The firefight lasted several minutes before four suspects were detained, three of them minors. Weapons seized included 6 long guns, 23 magazines, 549 rounds of ammunition, 4 bulletproof vests, 8 ballistic plates, and 1 ballistic helmet. The use of minor recruits reflects a documented pattern of cartel conscription of underage fighters in contested border zones, linked in part to CJNG infiltration from Nayarit. Escuinapa has now suffered multiple armed incidents within five days — a police ambush on March 31 (4 officers killed) and this army clash on April 5 — indicating an escalation of cartel activity in the Sinaloa–Nayarit corridor.
